message:
feat: Complete lint command implementation and wire into CLI dispatcher

This commit finalizes the lint command that was previously stubbed out,
enabling static analysis of issue descriptions for missing required sections.

main.rs:
- Replace stub implementation with actual lint::execute() dispatch
- Fix clippy lint: use String::new() instead of "".to_string() for
  empty string construction in undefer command

mod.rs:
- Remove WIP comment that indicated lint module was incomplete
- The LintArgs struct has been properly integrated into cli/mod.rs

lint.rs:
- Make exit_code() a const fn for compile-time evaluation capability
- Simplify exit_code logic: return 0 if json mode or no warnings,
  otherwise return 1 (same behavior, more idiomatic expression)
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] to RequiredSection.hint field - the hint
  is preserved for future use in providing user suggestions but is
  not currently read by any code path
- Refactor build_filters() to use struct initialization with defaults
  instead of creating default then mutating, satisfying clippy's
  preference for functional initialization patterns
- Make required_sections() a const fn since it only matches on enum
  variants and returns static references
- Simplify strip_heading_prefix() by extracting trimmed variable to
  avoid repeated .trim() calls

The lint command validates:
- Bug issues: require "## Steps to Reproduce" and "## Expected Behavior"
- Task/Feature issues: require "## Acceptance Criteria"
- Epic issues: require "## Acceptance Criteria" and "## Design"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
